title: bind-propagation -- shared -- not working
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: # Uncomment only one, leave it on its own line:

[//]: # **kind bug**

**Description**

Trying to use bind-propagation *shared* with podman does not work. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```
1. podman run --privileged -it --rm -v /root/owntmp:/owntmp:shared  fedora bash
2. [container] #  mount -o bind /usr /owntmp/usr
3. [container] #  ls /owntmp/usr
bin  games  include  lib  lib64  libexec  local  sbin  share  src  tmp
4. [host] # ls /root/owntmp/usr 
```
**Describe the results you received:**

An empty directory on the host. 

**Describe the results you expected:**

A populated directory on the host. 
`ls /root/owntmp/usr`  should give the same output as a `ls /owntmp/usr` in the container. 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Tested on RHEL-7.6, RHCOS-4.0

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Version:       0.10.1.3
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
ost:
  BuildahVersion: 1.5-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.10.1.3-1.gitdb08685.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 0e7abf050824203d775a20c26ed14f716f7d1aa7-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhcos"'
    version: "4.0"
  MemFree: 118853488640
  MemTotal: 128844967936
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-52.dev.git70ca035.el7_5.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 16
  hostname: ip-10-0-143-109
  kernel: 3.10.0-957.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 29h 22m 13.1s (Approximately 1.21 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: null
registries:
  registries:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 48
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 10
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @vrothberg Could you take a look at this?
Sure. I will look into this tomorrow.
I am not able to reproduce on openSUSE Tumbleweed. Neither with the reported version (i..e, 0.10.1.3) nor with the latest or HEAD. It behaves as expected. I will continue investigating.

Tested with latest HEAD, not working. 

`podman version 0.11.2-dev`
@giuseppe mentioned that he could reproduce on F29 ext4. I couldn't in my F28 XFS VM and will spin up a new F29 VM.

Since `rprivate` is the default and it is "working". Is anybody going to look at the other bind-propagation options left, besides `shared`? 

/cc @jeremyeder
I can reproduce. Grabbing another coffee helped.

@vrothberg do you see it also on openSUSE Tumbleweed now?

If not, can you tell me what do you get running `grep owntmp /proc/self/mountinfo` inside of the container?

> @vrothberg do you see it also on openSUSE Tumbleweed now?

Yes, I do. It was just a hiccup on my side.

It depends on `rootfsPropagation` that by default is set to `rslave`.

We should probably change the default to `slave`, something like:

```
diff --git a/libpod/container_internal_linux.go b/libpod/container_internal_linux.go
index 66c7e8a0..adbcab39 100644
--- a/libpod/container_internal_linux.go
+++ b/libpod/container_internal_linux.go
@@ -344,6 +344,8 @@ func (c *Container) generateSpec(ctx context.Context) (*spec.Spec, error) {
                g.SetLinuxCgroupsPath(cgroupPath)
        }
 
+       g.SetLinuxRootPropagation("slave")
+
        // Mounts need to be sorted so paths will not cover other paths
        mounts := sortMounts(g.Mounts())
        g.ClearMounts()
```

We set the default to that to match Docker. Should we only reset to slave
if we detect that a different propogation was requested?

On Wed, Nov 21, 2018, 08:13 Giuseppe Scrivano <notifications@github.com
wrote:

> It depends on rootfsPropagation that by default is set to rslave.
>
> We should probably change the default to slave, something like:
>
> diff --git a/libpod/container_internal_linux.go b/libpod/container_internal_linux.go
> index 66c7e8a0..adbcab39 100644
> --- a/libpod/container_internal_linux.go
> +++ b/libpod/container_internal_linux.go
> @@ -344,6 +344,8 @@ func (c *Container) generateSpec(ctx context.Context) (*spec.Spec, error) {
>                 g.SetLinuxCgroupsPath(cgroupPath)
>         }
>
> +       g.Config.Linux.RootfsPropagation = "slave"
> +
>         // Mounts need to be sorted so paths will not cover other paths
>         mounts := sortMounts(g.Mounts())
>         g.ClearMounts()
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1834#issuecomment-440657802>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCIZrKXlo69LTtHxuQTU_fU4FhChYks5uxVGQgaJpZM4YrICZ>
> .
>

I think Docker does that as well.  If there is any volume with `[r]shared` then it changes the rootfsPropagation to `shared`, differently, if there is any volume with `[r]slave`, it ensures the rootfsPropagation is at least `rslave`
@giuseppe `g.SetLinuxRootPropagation("slave")` can confirm, worked for me too.
Alright, looks like we should do something similar to https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/master/daemon/oci_linux.go#L527. I'll wrap up a PR and ping you there to have a look.

Was this fixed? 
```
kbaegis@crucible ~ $ sudo podman run -it --name nulllabs-build --privileged --network host --add-host services.lab:127.0.0.1 --add-host server.lab:127.0.0.1 --dns 127.0.0.1 --cap-add SYS_ADMIN --rm -v /var/lib/containers/:/var/lib/containers/:shared --entrypoint=/bin/bash crucible.lab:4000/oci/build:latest
WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
crucible / # cd /var/lib/containers/
crucible /var/lib/containers # ls
cache  storage
crucible /var/lib/containers # mkdir tmp
crucible /var/lib/containers # mkdir tmpbind
crucible /var/lib/containers # mount --bind tmp tmpbind/
mount: /var/lib/containers/tmpbind: bind /var/lib/containers/tmp failed.
crucible /var/lib/containers # cd
crucible ~ # mkdir tmp
crucible ~ # mkdir tmpbind
crucible ~ # mount --bind tmp tmpbind/
mount: /root/tmpbind: bind /root/tmp failed.
```

Version:            1.2.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.1
Git Commit:         3bd528e583182b4249f3e6bbd8497a8831d89950
Built:              Fri Apr 12 00:08:57 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

A word of advice unrelated to the actual bind propogation problem: If you want to mount /var/lib/containers into a container, we strongly recommend you also mount the host's /var/run as well - if you don't, c/storage and Podman can assume the system restarted and lose track of critical state (like what's mounted).

Will investigate bind propogation.
